title: Auditing and Shipping Events
description:
## Security Wants an Audit Trail

Security asks you to record **who logged in** (and failed logins) and **what administrators changed**, and to forward that activity to the company **SIEM**.

Which Keycloak feature satisfies this?

options.text: Enable **Login events** and **Admin events**, persist them, and attach an **Event Listener SPI** (e.g. one that forwards to the SIEM)
Rely only on the server's stdout **application logs** and grep them later
Turn on **brute force detection** — it logs every login and admin change
Increase the **access token lifespan** so sessions are easier to trace

explanation: Keycloak's **Events** subsystem is purpose-built for audit. **Login events** capture authentication activity (including failures), and **Admin events** capture administrative changes (optionally with the request representation). These can be **stored** and exposed via the Admin Console/REST API, and an **Event Listener SPI** can stream them to external systems such as a **SIEM**. (a) is correct. (b) server logs are unstructured and not a reliable audit source. (c) **brute force detection** only protects against repeated failed logins — it is not an audit/event-shipping mechanism. (d) token lifespan is a session-security setting and has nothing to do with auditing.

hints.title: Two event types + a listener
hints.body: Under Realm Settings → Events you toggle 'Login Events' and 'Admin Events' and register event listeners. A custom Event Listener provider is the hook to push events to a SIEM.
